title: symphytum tuberosum subsp. nodosum
intro: Symphytum tuberosum subsp. nodosum, also known as nodous comfrey, is a subspecies of the comfrey plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds, as well as its ability to stimulate tissue repair and promote wound healing. The health benefits of Symphytum tuberosum subsp. nodosum include reducing joint pain and inflammation, promoting bone growth, and aiding in the healing of wounds, fractures, and sprains. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used topically to accelerate wound healing and reduce pain.
